place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Has a sheltered basketball court that is used for everything except basketball. Ring is missing on one side of the court.

Had so much potential as one of the only few sheltered basketball courts in the area but unfortunately poorly maintained and not used to its potential.

Can you imagine a basketball court that is booked every evening (except Tuesdays) for non-basketball related activities? This is it.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs

review_text: Beautiful park in the middle of housing estate in Sengkang. Opened area with plenty of space for outdoor activities and picnics. There is a Tenderbest restaurant nearby. Clean and less crowded.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Love this park. Scenic. Bridges. Ponds. Chickens. Parrots. After a jog/walk, hop in to Tenderbest for a cuppa. Bliss.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Big open space. Terrapins roam free (don't feel, I guess and be careful of where you step). Also monitor lizards. Peaceful, air is so clean and fresh. Love the views and the vibes. Went in the evening so wasn't tortured by the sun.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Very nice relaxing walk not crowded. Prayed for bikes if you got them or you can rent them.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: it’s a great place for jogging. The paths are clean, well-maintained, and surrounded by lots of greenery

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Many stalls are closed or not rented out.. those that stays open have pretty good standard and choice available.
The hawker is airy and face a park, it’s windy and very serene in the morning and evening.
Some stalls with long que is the western grille and fish soup.  Can come for a beer or coffee to chillax.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Special mention to the gourmet mixed rice at level 1! Couple running the stall is very generous and nice, food is perfect for my palate (not too oily, spicy or salty)! Feels quite healthy. The most affordable and yummy caifan ever~ I always patron their stall when I’m around the area, and would go out of the way for it too :) I’ve been raving about their food a lot to my friends too, looove it so much!

$3.30 for this amount of food is insane (in a good way) in this economy…

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Sad place. Every other stall is up for rent. Some of the stalls with great reviews didn’t survive. At 7.30pm on a Friday night, the place is empty except 1 or 2 stalls with good reviews and still surviving, seen queue.  Rest of stalls, it’s just depressing.
Not worth the effort.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Huge spacious and clean. Pretty airy too.
Overall quite nice feel and vibes to it.
Saw quite afew stalls which looks good however tummy couldnt allow them all.

Not in my hood but will be back to try this the next time i'm near.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Go to the 2nd floor of this hawker center to find this Ramen gem. I ordered the $9.90 Sakura Ebi Premium Ramen and thoroughly enjoyed the rich umami flavour of the prawn stock soup. The noodles are firm and tasty, it comes with generous clams, scallops, charsiew, smoked duck and meatball. It's a serendipitous find!

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub (OTH) continues to be one of the most vibrant community spaces in Singapore. Even on a late weekday evening, the energy here is unmistakable. Here’s a review from my recent 8:30 PM visit.

🏮 Festive Vibes: Chinese New Year 2026

The Hub is currently decked out for Chinese New Year, and the atmosphere is fantastic.

• CNY Activities: There are plenty of festive displays and activities scattered throughout the ground floor, from zodiac-themed decorations to cultural showcases. It adds a wonderful touch of tradition to the modern architecture.

• The Crowd: Even at 8:30 PM, the place was quite crowded. It clearly remains the "heart" of Tampines, with families and residents soaking in the festive spirit late into the evening.

🎬 Entertainment: The Arena & Festive Plaza

One of the best features of OTH is the dedicated space for public viewing.

• Live Performances & Movies: The Festive Plaza (the large open atrium) is essentially an arena for the community. There’s almost always something on the "Big Ass Screen"—whether it's a live performance, a football match, or a free movie screening. It’s a great spot to just sit on the steps and unwind.

• The "Big Ass Fans": Despite being an open-air
concept, the cooling is impressive. The massive HVLS (High Volume Low Speed) fans—or "big ass fans" as many call them—keep the air circulating beautifully. Even in our humid weather, the breeze makes the space comfortable for hanging out.

🍛 Food & Convenience

If you're feeling hungry, you're never more than a few steps away from a good meal.

• The Hawker Centre: Located right next to the main festive areas, the OTH Hawker Centre is the perfect pitstop. It offers a massive variety of halal and non-halal stalls, making it incredibly convenient for a quick dinner after catching a show or finishing a workout.

Final Verdict

Our Tampines Hub is the gold standard for community spaces. Between the festive CNY activities and the breezy, comfortable "arena" area, it’s a great place to spend an evening.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
I like Purple Panda because the staff are friendly, patient.

I haven’t tried their food yet, but I’ve heard good things about it, so I’m looking forward to giving it a try next time.

The environment at Our Tampines Hub is also really nice and lively where there a lot of chairs area . I like the hawker area a lot, especially the seafood stalls all the way at the back it has a good variety

Overall, it’s a chill place with a nice vibe, good service, and a lot of food options around.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text: Tampines Hub (OTH) is truly an excellent community hub with a wide range of amenities under one roof. From the food court and sports facilities to live performances and a one-stop service centre, it offers great convenience for residents. The ample parking spaces also make visits hassle-free. A well-planned hub that caters to people of all ages and interests.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub truly feels like the heartbeat of the community, and it’s easy to understand why it’s known as Singapore’s largest community and lifestyle centre. The scale is impressive, but more than that, it reflects Singapore’s resilience and commitment to supporting community living in a meaningful way.

The architecture is beautiful, modern, and thoughtfully designed with sustainability in mind. Everything is conveniently housed in one place — hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, learning centres, indoor and outdoor running tracks, rooftop gardens, and sky gardens. The layout makes it easy to spend hours here without feeling overwhelmed, whether you’re running errands or just hanging out.

One of the standout highlights is the large central screen with seating around it. It creates a fantastic shared atmosphere for concerts, movie screenings, or watching live-streamed matches, especially when Singapore is being represented at major events like the Olympics. There’s also a football court that regularly hosts matches and other events, adding to the vibrant energy of the space.

Families are well catered for too, with a climbing wall and dedicated play areas for children. The large auditorium, which seats around 1,500 people, is another impressive feature, often hosting performances and community events. Essential government services like Service SG are also located here, making it incredibly convenient. Even though it gets busy in the evenings, the lively footfall just shows how well-loved and actively used Our Tampines Hub really is.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
📍Level 2, Our Tampines Hub

👏🏻 Sheltered playground alert 👏🏻

Ah, this one holds a special place in our heart as my kid grew up at this playground. From stumbling around to confidently running full tilt at the trampoline now.

👧🏻 Toddler verdict: 8/10. The shelter is a big plus point in our tropical weather. This huge area is padded and comes complete with many toddler-friendly zones

👉 Parents’ note: There’s one more discreet (and sheltered) playground at level 4 if this one gets crowded

Follow us @slideseekersg on Instagram for more playground reviews!

From the food court and sports facilities to live performances and a one-stop service centre, it offers great convenience for residents. The ample parking spaces also make visits hassle-free. A well-planned hub that caters to people of all ages and interests.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub truly feels like the heartbeat of the community, and it’s easy to understand why it’s known as Singapore’s largest community and lifestyle centre. The scale is impressive, but more than that, it reflects Singapore’s resilience and commitment to supporting community living in a meaningful way.

The architecture is beautiful, modern, and thoughtfully designed with sustainability in mind. Everything is conveniently housed in one place — hawker centres, food courts, restaurants, learning centres, indoor and outdoor running tracks, rooftop gardens, and sky gardens. The layout makes it easy to spend hours here without feeling overwhelmed, whether you’re running errands or just hanging out.

One of the standout highlights is the large central screen with seating around it. It creates a fantastic shared atmosphere for concerts, movie screenings, or watching live-streamed matches, especially when Singapore is being represented at major events like the Olympics. There’s also a football court that regularly hosts matches and other events, adding to the vibrant energy of the space.

Families are well catered for too, with a climbing wall and dedicated play areas for children. The large auditorium, which seats around 1,500 people, is another impressive feature, often hosting performances and community events. Essential government services like Service SG are also located here, making it incredibly convenient. Even though it gets busy in the evenings, the lively footfall just shows how well-loved and actively used Our Tampines Hub really is.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
📍Level 2, Our Tampines Hub

👏🏻 Sheltered playground alert 👏🏻

Ah, this one holds a special place in our heart as my kid grew up at this playground. From stumbling around to confidently running full tilt at the trampoline now.

👧🏻 Toddler verdict: 8/10. The shelter is a big plus point in our tropical weather. This huge area is padded and comes complete with many toddler-friendly zones

👉 Parents’ note: There’s one more discreet (and sheltered) playground at level 4 if this one gets crowded

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Our Tampines Hub (OTH) continues to be one of the most vibrant community spaces in Singapore. Even on a late weekday evening, the energy here is unmistakable. Here’s a review from my recent 8:30 PM visit.

🏮 Festive Vibes: Chinese New Year 2026

The Hub is currently decked out for Chinese New Year, and the atmosphere is fantastic.

• CNY Activities: There are plenty of festive displays and activities scattered throughout the ground floor, from zodiac-themed decorations to cultural showcases. It adds a wonderful touch of tradition to the modern architecture.

• The Crowd: Even at 8:30 PM, the place was quite crowded. It clearly remains the "heart" of Tampines, with families and residents soaking in the festive spirit late into the evening.

🎬 Entertainment: The Arena & Festive Plaza

One of the best features of OTH is the dedicated space for public viewing.

• Live Performances & Movies: The Festive Plaza (the large open atrium) is essentially an arena for the community. There’s almost always something on the "Big Ass Screen"—whether it's a live performance, a football match, or a free movie screening. It’s a great spot to just sit on the steps and unwind.

• The "Big Ass Fans": Despite being an open-air
concept, the cooling is impressive. The massive HVLS (High Volume Low Speed) fans—or "big ass fans" as many call them—keep the air circulating beautifully. Even in our humid weather, the breeze makes the space comfortable for hanging out.

🍛 Food & Convenience

If you're feeling hungry, you're never more than a few steps away from a good meal.

• The Hawker Centre: Located right next to the main festive areas, the OTH Hawker Centre is the perfect pitstop. It offers a massive variety of halal and non-halal stalls, making it incredibly convenient for a quick dinner after catching a show or finishing a workout.

Final Verdict

Our Tampines Hub is the gold standard for community spaces. Between the festive CNY activities and the breezy, comfortable "arena" area, it’s a great place to spend an evening.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Decent spot for variety, but not for those craving familiar Pakistani flavours
Visited Lau Pa Sat (Lay Pa Sat) recently in Singapore. It’s a lively and iconic hawker centre with plenty of options and a nice atmosphere, especially in the evening when the streets are closed off for dining.
We tried the halal stall and ordered chicken rice noodles, chicken basil vegetables, and tea tree something. While the portions were generous and the service was quick, the overall taste was quite different from what we expected. Everything had a strong seafood-like flavour profile (even the non-seafood items), which didn’t really appeal to us as I can’t stand sea food smell. It felt quite far from Pakistani taste preferences – the spices, seasoning, and balance were very different.
If you’re in the mood to explore local Singaporean or Southeast Asian hawker food and try new things, Lau Pa Sat is a great place with lots of variety. However, if you’re particular about your food and want something closer to Pakistani cuisine, this might not be the best choice.
Cleanliness was just fine for a busy hawker centre, and the pricing was reasonable. Good for tourists or casual explorers, but we probably won’t rush back.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: fantastic place to grab some food and/or beer. Famous for the skewers on the street, especially the chicken satay. Plenty of options to choose from, Indian, Arabic, Chinese, Singaporean, etc. There's something for everyone. Good ventilation and plenty of tables but the place is always packed and noisy (it doesn't take away the enjoyment). A must to visit.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: There's no need to write much about this place; you simply must stop in for a fantastic meal. There are plenty of street food joints offering a variety of Asian cuisines. Everything is perfectly seasoned, with plenty to choose from, so everyone should easily find something to their liking. And the prices are very affordable by Singaporean standards.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Lau Pa Sat: A must-visit for the lively night vibe!

I visited at 9 PM on a Friday, and it was absolutely crowded! The energy outside on Satay Street was incredible, with everyone enjoying grilled food in the open air. Inside the historic building, there are plenty of food stalls to choose from.

The Drinks:
One thing to watch out for is that there are very few drink stalls, so the lines are very long. I bought an Iced Mango Peach ($2.80), but I wouldn't recommend it. It was extremely sweet and didn't contain any fresh fruit—it tasted mostly like syrup. I suggest skipping the "juices" here if you want something fresh.

The Food & Service:
I tried the Thunder Tea Rice ($6.20). It was okay, though it didn't quite fit my personal taste. After eating, the service is efficient; you can either clear your own tray or wait for the staff who come by frequently to collect them.

The Verdict:
It is very crowded and noisy with loud music, but it’s a "must-visit" to experience the local Singaporean vibe. Just be prepared for the crowds!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Tried Na Na Curry at Lau Pa Sat because saw that they are awarded Michelin Bib Gourmand 10 years in a row!

Their chicken curry is rich, slightly creamy, and super comforting. The spices are well-balanced, not too heavy but still very satisfying.

The chicken is tender and soaks up the curry nicely, making every bite flavourful. Pairs really well with both the rice and roti prata!

review_text:
Pretty impressed with Pasir Ris Mall! It is modern, well-designed, and easy to navigate. There’s a wide variety of restaurants and eateries to satisfy everyone’s tastes. Kids will also have a great time here, thanks to the spacious arcade and dedicated play areas.

The mall even features an outdoor space with greenery on the second level, adding a refreshing touch.
Its amenities seem endless — integrated with a Polyclinic, an air-conditioned bus interchange, and direct access to the MRT.

Altogether, it truly feels like a complete one-stop destination for shopping, dining, entertainment, and lifestyle needs.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Pasir Ris Mall — The New Heart of the East

Standing beside Pasir Ris MRT and Bus Interchange, Pasir Ris Mall is the newest lifestyle destination in Singapore’s eastern coast — a modern community hub where urban living meets coastal charm.

Opened in 2025, the mall is part of the larger Pasir Ris 8 integrated development, developed by Allgreen Properties and Kerry Properties. Designed as a seamless link between home, transport, and recreation, it offers residents and visitors everything within reach — from fashion and dining to wellness, groceries, and family entertainment.

Architecturally, Pasir Ris Mall draws inspiration from the nearby park and sea. Its design blends natural light, open terraces, and lush greenery, creating a calm and airy environment that mirrors the laid-back rhythm of the East. Within minutes, visitors can stroll to Pasir Ris Park or the beach — making it a rare retail space that connects urban convenience with nature’s serenity.

More than just a shopping centre, Pasir Ris Mall is envisioned as a social heart — where people gather, families unwind, and community life thrives. It reflects Singapore’s new urban design philosophy: integrated, sustainable, and people-centred.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Fully air-conditioned bus interchange with shops, similar to Toa Payoh Bus Interchange.

Lets dive in!

Newly opened bus interchange in Pasir Ris connected to Pasir Ris Mall. This is one interchange we love to come back to. The cold air and shops around, made us feel that the mall didnt end.

Do note that there are at least 2 ijooz vending Machines. So u guys can get your orange juice fix without much of a queue. At the same time, there are also a small canteen-like makan area here.

With Swee Heng and many others on board, we really were able to get spme resources before boarding the bus. Although it hasnt fully opened, its a great place to go to.

All in all, if you are near, go on and enjoy the amenities the bus interchange offers. Oh yes, we are not taking photos from outside, since there are not much effects for prominence here.

Cheers!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
They tried their best to upgrade the bus interchange.

Also the new aircon bus interchange does not have any entrance to the MRT station and people have to walk a large round to enter the bus interchange, often getting wet from rain and also flooding which makes our shoes wet and unable to focus on work after reaching office.

However perhaps could consider passenger volume for different bus and adjust the frequency.

Due to for bus 17, most people take it and it’s always long queue , but the frequency for this bus is much longer than bus 5/6/12 (which much fewer passengers take it). As these bus stops in the middle of nowhere and only serves industrial worker who don’t take it often outside office hours.

If you just stay for afew hours during peak hours of weekday and weekend , you can notice these trends.

Thanks alot 🙏

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
The newly opened Pasir Ris Bus Interchange is a great transport hub offering quick access to the mall and MRT station.
It boasts excellent facilities and conveniences, including a small food area.
The air-conditioned comfort is definitely a plus for commuters.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
Fully air-conditioned bus interchange with shops, similar to Toa Payoh Bus Interchange.

Lets dive in!

Newly opened bus interchange in Pasir Ris connected to Pasir Ris Mall. This is one interchange we love to come back to. The cold air and shops around, made us feel that the mall didnt end.

Do note that there are at least 2 ijooz vending Machines. So u guys can get your orange juice fix without much of a queue. At the same time, there are also a small canteen-like makan area here.

With Swee Heng and many others on board, we really were able to get spme resources before boarding the bus. Although it hasnt fully opened, its a great place to go to.

All in all, if you are near, go on and enjoy the amenities the bus interchange offers. Oh yes, we are not taking photos from outside, since there are not much effects for prominence here.

Cheers!

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
New bus interchange fully air conditioned
But still need to open the main link to mrt n interchange
Current exit gate 3 is not ok if heavy rain or raining or sunny shelter is missing n difficult to reach mrt during this time else need to go round via Pasir Ris new mall
Also now it’s far from White Sands mall
Opening the gate between the mrt n interchange or the new mall will be more convenient
358 is other buses are more frequently accessible from mrt to city but the gates for such buses were far from mrt gates
Waiting for final finishing

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text:
They tried their best to upgrade the bus interchange.

Also the new aircon bus interchange does not have any entrance to the MRT station and people have to walk a large round to enter the bus interchange, often getting wet from rain and also flooding which makes our shoes wet and unable to focus on work after reaching office.

However perhaps could consider passenger volume for different bus and adjust the frequency.

Due to for bus 17, most people take it and it’s always long queue , but the frequency for this bus is much longer than bus 5/6/12 (which much fewer passengers take it). As these bus stops in the middle of nowhere and only serves industrial worker who don’t take it often outside office hours.

If you just stay for afew hours during peak hours of weekday and weekend , you can notice these trends.

Thanks alot 🙏

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: A peaceful escape from the city! Perfect for a scenic jog, bike ride, or just a relaxing stroll by the water.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Quiet, underrated gem in the northeast. Punggol Park has a really chill vibe, especially around the big lake in the middle. Great for evening walks, light jogs, or just sitting down to unwind after a long day.

Nice mix of facilities too. There’s a Tenderbest nearby for easy bites, a gym pod if you want a quick workout, plus water coolers and vending machines around for convenience. You’ll also spot exercise stations catered for the elderly, which is a thoughtful touch. There is a nice woodball corner as well and also an open area for football.

Plenty of shaded paths and open spaces, so it never feels too crowded. You’ll see families, cyclists, and even people fishing by the pond. Simple, not overly fancy, but that’s part of the charm.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
If u are in Hougang / Sengkang area, and wanted a park to stroll or exercise.  Try this Punggol Park.
Pls note that although it called Punggol Park, it’s actually in end of upper Serangoon, Hougang area.
It’s a smaller to Bedok Reservior Park, but it’s wide enough for most outdoor exercise.
Peaceful and tranquil.  U can have a drink/ food at the eatery: Tenderbest.  They have both indoor AC and alfresco dining area.

Also, if u want to walk/ cycle more, it’s just 5mins walk away to the Serangoon Park Connector.  Which will lead u to Kallang southwards or Northwards will lead u to Punggol Point or Changi Pt via Pasir Ris PCN.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Actually, It's a Nice and quiet park with a running loop around a pond.The park with a lot of place to rest chair benches. It's shady with huge trees and bird chirping. The place is Great for walking, jogging or biking. You can come here anytime u want some peace and to de- stress through a nice and scenic walk in the park. At Night time the lights around the pond makes for a cool background. Overall a good place to bring the family and have some exercise. 🌳🌱🌿🍂🍃🍀🪺🌸💮🐦🐦‍⬛🚴🏃🚶🧎🤸

review_text: attended a 3 day wake at the oratory of the Church of Nativity recently. On one of the evenings, I went for a walk in the park. It was pleasant to be in nature, to reflect on the life. place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Pasir Ris Town Park, which serves as a wonderful recreational hub for the community. The park centers around a large marine fishing pond, making it an ideal destination for anglers and fishing enthusiasts to practice their skills.

In addition to its appeal for fishing, the park is excellent for residents looking to stay physically active, as it features a dedicated Fitness Corner and a paved, flat jogging track. Visitors can also enjoy a variety of dining options within the park itself, including a hawker centre, various eateries, and a bistro, providing a great setting for an evening meal by the water.

Accessibility to the park is very convenient; it is well-connected by two bridges crossing Sungei Tampines and via the Park Connector. Furthermore, Pasir Ris MRT Station is located just a few minutes away, making it easily accessible for everyone.

It truly is a fantastic space for residents and visitors alike.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: This is a beautiful park along the sea shore, accessible from the Pasir Ris MRT Station. There are lots of trees and you can see the occasional ships passing by. There are lots of small white yachts on the water, there must be be a sailing school somewhere. The sea breeze is cooling and pleasant, even in the mid afternoon, making this a tranquil and relaxing place to come and spend the day. You can also visit the nearby mangrove swamps and walk along the boardwalk.

Visitors can also enjoy a variety of dining options within the park itself, including a hawker centre, various eateries, and a bistro, providing a great setting for an evening meal by the water.

Accessibility to the park is very convenient; it is well-connected by two bridges crossing Sungei Tampines and via the Park Connector. Furthermore, Pasir Ris MRT Station is located just a few minutes away, making it easily accessible for everyone.

It truly is a fantastic space for residents and visitors alike.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
There is a special Childcare Centre - Loyang Point Kinderland Child Care Centre. ​From the outside, you might not see it, but inside this centre is a place bursting with life, love, and learning. It’s a Centre of dedication, led by the incredibly humble and respectful Principal, Ms. Mei Fong. She is a true educator at heart, always communicating with parents and ensuring that we as parents/guardians are well-informed. She is always courteous, helpful and knowledgeable.

​I am consistently impressed by the entire team, from the lady who takes temperatures each morning to the teachers and Principal—they all know every child by name. This center turns every small moment into a learning opportunity, from encouraging kids to put on their own shoes to teaching them to carry their haversacks and personal hygiene. This hands-on guidance truly helps our children build skills and values every single day.

​It's clear that the teachers here genuinely love the children. As a parent whose child was once an infant here, I saw firsthand the level of care given. They pay close attention to every detail, from a child's appetite and health to teaching them new social and developmental skills through fun activities. My baby returns home every workday with clean diapers, well fed , changed clothings and happy.

​A special shout-out and deepest appreciation to the teachers who have had a profound impact on my child's journey: Principal Mei Fong, Teacher Nira, Teacher Florence, Teacher Fizah, and Ms. Wang. Talents like this is the heart of any successful school. We are so lucky to have such a dedicated and compassionate team. We hope their incredible contributions are recognized and rewarded, ensuring they stay to nurture future generations of children.

​Thank you for everything you do!

PS:
Unfortunately we have to leave this school as we have moved house some 45 minutes away.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My child loves going to this preschool! The teachers are so caring and friendly, and they make learning really fun. I can see how much confidence and joy my little one has gained since starting here. Such a warm and happy place for kids!

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: My twins joined K1 in Jul 2024. We have seen the progress in them, in speaking out. Would say they are systematic in their education curriculum. Attentive Principal Ms Mei Fong around most of the time to ensure all is well. Thank you to the Team of Teachers and Staff. They really try their best.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
As a working parent, finding the right preschool for your child is crucial as the children would spend most of their time when we are working. We couldn't be happier with our decision to enroll our child at Kinderland at Loyang Point. All my 3 kids grow up in Kinderland at Loyang Point.

The teachers and staff in Kinderland at Loyang Point are genuinely caring about each child well being.

Their curriculum is thoughtfully designed to stimulate young minds. They incorporate music into learning to make learning fun and educational.

The facilities are well-maintained and clean, which create a confident knowing that our child is in a secure environment.

Communication with the preschool is excellent. The teachers keep us informed about our child's progress and any developments in the classroom. They also encourage parental involvement through events, workshops, and open communication channels.

In conclusion, they have exceeded our expectations in every way. It's clear that they prioritize the well-being and development of each child in their care and we are very happy that our kids have grown up well in their care.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
My First Impressions of Tampines Eco Green Park: A Mini Forest Park in Tampines Town

Experience nature just a stone's throw away from the bustling urban environment of Tampines.

This tranquil park is the ideal destination for anyone looking to connect with nature.

*What is Tampines Eco Green Park? - Mini Forest Park!**

Tampines Eco Green is a 36.5-hectare park, offering a rare glimpse of nearly untouched nature in this urban area. Unlike Singapore’s more manicured parks, Tampines Eco Green features marshlands, secondary forests (mini forest park) and freshwater ponds. Opened in 2011, it has become a favoured spot for bird-watchers, nature enthusiasts, and anyone seeking a peaceful retreat without venturing far from home. Residents in the surrounding neighborhoods can truly appreciate this natural oasis.

**Where is mini forest park?**
This triangular park is situated between Tampines Avenue 9 and 12, bordered by the Tampines Expressway and the Sungei Tampines canal. With entrances located along Avenue 12 and via Sun Plaza Park, it is easily accessible, and it connects seamlessly to the broader park connector network for those traveling on foot or by bicycle.

This mini forest features three walking trails and boasts a rich biodiversity. Nestled at the edge of Tampines town, this green space offers joggers and walkers a rustic escape just moments away from the urban hustle.

It’s truly a hidden gem, featuring well-maintained pathways that provide a peaceful passage into nature. The early morning tranquility, before the day's activities commence, is particularly enchanting. Whether you are a nature lover or simply in search of a refreshing start to your day, this park merits a visit. Residents of nearby HDB blocks or those just across from the park will greatly appreciate this natural retreat.

Come and immerse yourself in the beauty of nature!

Chris
30 May 2026

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: A decent spot for a quiet stroll, but nothing particularly special. It's quite small, just ~3km of  basic grass trails that felt repetitive. It's okay for a quick 40-minute loop, but don't expect  amenities like paved paths, cafes, playgrounds or carparks, since it is designed as a minimum intervention sanctuary for wildlife.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I love the rustic feel during the walk. It's a small but good place to escape the concrete jungle.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Tampines Eco Park: A Brilliant Morning Awakening with Nature's Wonders (Sunscreen & Mozzie Spray Essential!)

My early morning walk through Tampines Eco Park was an absolute delight, a vibrant start to the day under the brilliant Singaporean sun. The ground, still soft and yielding from the previous night's rain, released a lovely, earthy fragrance into the fresh air.

The park was already teeming with life. Numerous roosters and hens busily foraged, embodying the early bird adage. The pond was equally active, with sleek monitor lizards gracefully swimming and others basking lazily on the banks, their scales shimmering in the sunlight. It was a captivating glimpse into the park's diverse wildlife.

To fully enjoy this natural haven, a couple of tips are essential: don't forget your sunscreen, as even the early morning sun can be strong in Singapore. Additionally, a good lemongrass-based mosquito repellent is highly recommended to keep those pesky insects at bay, allowing you to fully immerse yourself in the park's tranquility.

Tampines Eco Park is a true gem, offering well-maintained paths for a peaceful escape into nature. The early morning serenity, before the daytime bustle, is particularly special. Whether you're a nature lover or simply seeking a refreshing start to your day, this park is well worth a visit.

Highly Recommended for Tampines Residents

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
🌳🌿Tampines Eco Green, unlike other typical heartland Park, is a ecological Park set in a totally natural setting with unique landscape which include marshland, freshwater ponds, open grassland and forested area.
🐢🐠🐙The Park's three natural trails, Diversity Trail, Marsh Trail, Forest Trail, wound around the entire Eco park, leading visitors to diverse natural setting, including various strategically located bird hides and freshwater ponds.
🌿🐦🦅The Park is best enjoyed for bird watching and being close to nature.
🧺🚽🧹Interestingly, the only toilet at Tampines Eco Green is a eco-toilet that has no flushing, but uses biodegrading to turn human waste to compost.
🎄The ecological Park is a welcome deviation from the usual Park.
🍂🌿Very tranquil and idyllic park, except for the occasional MRT trains.

review_text: ​The park's main activities are conveniently clustered around McDonald's, which serves as a great central spot for dining, socializing, and recreation. Aside from active play, it is a fantastic place for gatherings, featuring camping grounds, barbecue pits, large open lawns for picnics, kite flying and jogging.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
West Coast Park is a long but relatively quiet park that stretches along Singapore’s western coastline. It’s a wonderful place for a relaxed outdoor day, especially for families with kids

One of the highlights is the large playground, which is one of the better ones in Singapore. The climbing structures, slides, and play areas keep children entertained for a long time, and there’s plenty of open space nearby for running around

The park is also great for cycling and casual walks. You can easily rent bicycles or family pedal carts near the entrance and ride along the park connectors while enjoying the sea breeze

For those who enjoy spending more time outdoors, the park has BBQ pits and camping areas, making it perfect for weekend gatherings with friends and family. There are also scenic spots along the waterfront where people fish or simply relax and watch the ships in the distance

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: I do like to come to this nature park by the sea - to take in the salty air and the cool sea breeze, both of which relaxes and then invigorates me. I like to hear the waves crashing against the shore and to be among the greenery in this nature park - in particular, the bamboo fringed walkway as regards the latter. The amenities at this nature park is adequate - with enough car parking spaces and public toilets. And overall, this nature park is well-maintained.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: Well maintained park and a good place to spend time alone or with family and friends. Suitable for all ages, whether to exercise in the mornings or catch a lovely sunset in the evenings.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
West Coast Park is considerably smaller than East Coast Park (ECP), but it remains a highly viable zone for R&R. I have scouted the perimeter and identified several optimal coordinates with good sightlines to hold position and decompress.

Civilian population density in this grid is minimal. The decreased foot traffic results in a secure perimeter and a highly tranquil atmosphere—ideal for lowering your guard and recovering stamina.

Be advised on two minor variables:

First, visuals on the maritime border are compromised and do not match the aesthetics of ECP.

Second, heavy transport vessels are active in the adjacent waters. My MJOLNIR armor’s environmental sensors occasionally detected industrial exhaust drifting into the area. It is non-lethal, but noticeable without a helmet filtration system.

Overall assessment: A solid, quiet sector for downtime. Proceed to coordinates at your discretion.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
My go-to neighbourhood mall whenever I need to get my errands done—super convenient and has pretty much everything I need in one place.

I especially love the public library there, it’s such a lovely spot to just slow down and unwind. There are always plenty of new books to browse, and the space feels calm and inviting.

The staff are also really friendly, which makes the whole experience even nicer. Definitely a place I keep coming back to 🤍

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: The first shopping centre at Pasir Ris .It still holds that familiar charm with many of the old  tenants still there. As it is just next to the mrt station, it remains an important food location for those coming back from school or work.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
I used to enjoy eating at White Sands Basement 1, especially the fishball noodle stall beside Twyst Pasta. Unfortunately, the experience has become very unpleasant because of the extremely strong and irritating fumes coming from the pasta stall whenever they cook.

The smoke and chilli smell are so strong that many people walking past start coughing immediately. Even customers sitting nearby, neighbouring stall workers, and people across the dining area are visibly affected. I have personally seen elderly customers coughing repeatedly because of the fumes.

What disappointed me most was the staff attitude. While people around were coughing from the strong fumes, one staff member appeared to mock the coughing and laugh about it instead of showing concern. It felt very unprofessional and inconsiderate towards customers sharing the public dining area.

I understand cooking produces smells, but this situation has been ongoing for a long time and seriously affects the overall dining environment. Sadly, because of this experience, I no longer feel comfortable coming here to eat.

review_text: Great mall with plenty of eateries and lifestyle shops as well as great connectivity to Mrt and bus interchange. We were look for cakes to celeb a birthday and there of plenty of choices from plenty of bakeries here. We were happy visiting here yesterday.

The smoke and chilli smell are so strong that many people walking past start coughing immediately. Even customers sitting nearby, neighbouring stall workers, and people across the dining area are visibly affected. I have personally seen elderly customers coughing repeatedly because of the fumes.

What disappointed me most was the staff attitude. While people around were coughing from the strong fumes, one staff member appeared to mock the coughing and laugh about it instead of showing concern. It felt very unprofessional and inconsiderate towards customers sharing the public dining area.

I understand cooking produces smells, but this situation has been ongoing for a long time and seriously affects the overall dining environment. Sadly, because of this experience, I no longer feel comfortable coming here to eat.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Dear Management Team,
I am writing to share a truly exceptional experience I recently had at your store.
While shopping for meat at GIANT Food, I was warmly welcomed by an outstanding employee whose name tag identified him as Mr. Youn Sang. From the moment I approached the counter, he made my entire experience feel special and valued.
What impressed me most was his attentiveness to everyone around him. No customer in line was left unattended. Mothers with children were acknowledged with patience and kindness, and every single person received a warm, personalized greeting. His professionalism, positive energy, and genuine care for customers truly stood out.
It is rare to encounter someone who takes such pride in delivering exceptional service. Mr. Youn Sang made my shopping experience not only pleasant but memorable, and I felt it was important that your management team be made aware of the remarkable job he is doing.
Please extend my sincere appreciation to him. Employees like Mr. Youn Sang are a tremendous asset to your organization.
Sincerely,
Jay.C

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Worst hypermarket experience. Only three checkout counters were open on a Saturday night, which naturally led to long queues. We waited about 40 minutes just to check out. If the store is short-staffed, it should reconsider operating 24 hours. Giant, please learn from Sheng Siong.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: This is one of the best mega warehouses in Singapore to shop on the East side as it carries many brands of food, household n electrical items . The layout is good where one can shop in comfort. There is a food court that has a nice range of food where one can indulge after the shopping. A great place to stock up essentials for home as the ample parking is convenient to trolley ones purchase to the car. One can find wonderful bargains as the store holds promotional product brands that are a steal. Happy shopping , happy family.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text: Today, January 12, 2026, at the Giant food court, I ordered *mee hoon* soto from the Nasi Padang stall, asking for an extra chicken add-on for $2.00. After I collected the food, it was not hot and contained only about one tablespoon of chicken. I asked the lady, who sounded Indonesian, about it. She was not happy when I asked her if the $2.00 chicken had been added. She pulled the bowl, and not wanting to create a scene, I just pulled it back and left. I'm very disappointed with her attitude; at the very least, she could have explained nicely. This will be the last time.

place_query_type: shopping groceries malls supermarkets markets
review_text:
Final Giant Hypemarket at Tampines ✔️✔️✔️✔️✔️👍👍👍

Giant Hypermarket at Tampines is ideal for one-stop grocery needs if you live in this area. There is a wide range of electronics, kitchenware, groceries, a semi-wet market, fruits, and vegetables. The prices are reasonable. There are also food stalls serving plenty of local dishes. Halal and vegetarian options are available and typically served until around 9:30 PM. Additionally, there is ample parking space with free parking available. If you live in this area, it’s an excellent place to shop for your daily needs.
Chris
17 Sept 2025 11am

place_name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Pasir Ris (Pasir Ris Mall) | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5
review_text:
My son recently joined the centre, and I am so glad to see how much he enjoys his time there!

Initially, he was very resistant to the idea of attending a student care centre and preferred to stay home alone. However, the warmth and dedication of the staff and teachers quickly helped him feel welcomed and comfortable. The science experiment activities conducted during the school holiday programme were a particular highlight for him — he came home excitedly sharing what he had learned and was genuinely looking forward to returning the next day.

I would also like to express my appreciation to Lydia, who was incredibly patient, responsive, and supportive throughout the enquiry and enrolment process. She took the time to address my many questions thoughtfully and professionally. What I value most is that Lydia and her team place students' growth, well-being, and psychological safety at the heart of everything they do.

Thank you, Lydia and team, for creating such a nurturing and engaging environment for the children. We are truly grateful for the positive impact you have made on my son's experience.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
place_name: MindChamps MindSpace @ Pasir Ris (Pasir Ris Mall) | After School Student Care for P1-P5 | MindChamps Enrichment for P1-P5
review_text:
Many parents, including myself, may initially be skeptical about the value MindSpace can provide given its high school fees. I too had reservations, especially considering that the fee is significantly higher compared to general after-school care. However, after attending a sharing session from the center, I decided to give it a chance.

My children's academic results were not satisfactory, and we lacked the time to help them with their studies in the evenings, especially considering how tired they were after a long day. Having enrichment classes during their time at after-school care has allowed them to use their time more purposefully than simply playing around without a structured program. This also frees up our weekends, allowing us to engage in interactive activities with our children instead of shuttling them from one enrichment class to another.

After being at MindSpace for over three months, we have already noticed improvements in their academic performance. We are still monitoring their progress and hope to see continuous improvement, ultimately leading to better grades by the end of the year.

In my opinion, it's worthwhile to give MindSpace a try if you are able to manage the fees.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Heartbeat@Bedok is more than just a building; it’s a beautifully designed, integrated lifestyle hub that truly feels like the pulse of the neighborhood. Whether you're there for a workout, a meal, or just to pass the time, it offers a welcoming atmosphere for all ages.

What Makes it Special:

• Airy & Spacious Design: The architecture is a standout. It features high ceilings and clever natural ventilation that keeps the entire complex feeling breezy and airy, even on warmer days. The lush greenery integrated into the terraces makes it feel like an urban forest.

• A Foodie’s Haven: You’ll never go hungry here. There are lots of restaurants and cafes ranging from popular chains like Sushi Express and Burger King to the One Two Eat food court, which serves up a wide variety of local and international cuisines.

• Plenty of Seating: One of the best parts about this hub is the abundance of public space. There are plenty of seats scattered throughout the levels, especially near the library and community areas, making it easy to find a spot to rest.

• Stay Active: For those looking for some friendly competition, there are table tennis tables (TTX) available for public use. It’s a great way for people who want to play a quick game to engage with others in the community.

• Community & Creativity: The building houses a large Community Centre and a modern Public Library. There’s even a dedicated handicraft corner and various interest group rooms where residents can pick up new skills or work on DIY projects.

👵 The Perfect Senior Hangout
Heartbeat@Bedok is arguably one of the best places for the elderlies to hangout. With the senior care center and polyclinic located on-site, the environment is very senior-friendly (ramps, elevators, and large-print books in the library). It’s common to see groups of seniors having a chat over coffee or sitting in the airy common areas, making it a very inclusive and social space.

place_query_type: recreation sports gyms swimming community clubs
review_text:
Had a great time at the Singapore Police Force Community Roadshow 2025 @ Bedok! 🎉 It's clear why I'm quite keen on visiting Heartbeat @ Bedok—it was the place to be for this exciting event.
I was so glad I decided to visit Heartbeat @ Bedok today! Not only is it a great community hub, but it was also the venue of the FINAL DAY of the fantastic Police Community Roadshow (PCR) 2025.

The atmosphere was buzzing with energy! The highlight for me was definitely the fantastic line-up of activities:
• 🎶 Singapore Police Force Band Performance: I got to catch the amazing Police Force Band, which was truly captivating! (The video I took shows the "Bringing Beat to You Since 1925" segment on the screen!)
• 🐕 Thrilling Police K-9 Show: The Police K-9 Show was impressive, demonstrating the skill and discipline of our police dogs and their handlers.
• 🚨 Exclusive Collectible: The main reason I was so excited was that I successfully registered to redeem a Die-Cast Police Vehicle! It's an awesome collectible and a great souvenir from the event.

It was a wonderful opportunity to see the Police Force engage with the community and showcase their work. Events like this make Heartbeat @ Bedok a truly vibrant and special place to visit!

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Many stalls were closed when I reached there today @ 3.50pm ( 9 May 2026, Saturday ) . It’s a medium size hawker centre. Many drinks stalls . I went there specifically for the famous Ivy’s Hainanese Herbal Mutton Soup but my own fault that I didn’t check their operation hours and they were closed at 3pm.

As not many stalls were open at the time I was I there so not many choices. I went for the fish soup and it was good 👍🏻.

Also tried the famous rojak . It was good 👍🏻 too.

The tables were clean.

The food centre is well ventilated with many fans installed overhead. So it was windy and comfortable eating there. Flies appear around 6+pm though. Not sure why.

There were many customers at 4pm. More customers when I left the food centre at 7pm. Many workers in technicians uniforms came in for their dinner 🍽️ .

I will definitely return for the Hainanese Herbal Mutton Soup on another day.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Hawkers centre is very clean. Floor is almost spotless. Tables are cleared and cleaned. Not many stalls opened on a Sat night. Had the seafood soup. Didnt enjoy it as it had a bad fishy after taste.

The fruit rojak was good and priced reasonably. Drinks aunty was very friendly.

The place was not hot as there were fans and it was well ventilated.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: A spacious spot with plenty of seating, though it can get a little hot during midday. The curry rice is tasty and satisfying — definitely worth trying! The friendly aunty at the stall makes the experience even better, and she accepts all sorts of payment methods, making it super convenient.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Good hawker centre right next to MRT station with plenty of good choices at reasonable prices.

There’s a segregation of Malay Indian stalls and a non-halal local side.

Favourites are Jubilant chicken rice!
Many other stalls like roasts, BCM fishball noodles are highly rated.

Also a Japanese ramen with high ratings!

Good coffee too!

I found prices are generally lower here than other hawkers in the city.

Only gripe is the amount of smokers who stood or sit around the hawker centre to smoke. Sometimes the smoke comes right to the seated eating areas.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Cosy clean hawker centre with a variety of local favourites including my beloved char kway teow (unfortunately was closed today) and a Michelin bib Hainanese herbal mutton soup

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
Woodlands Waterfront Park is a relaxing and scenic park located in the northern part of Singapore. It faces the Straits of Johor, so visitors can actually see Malaysia across the water. The park covers around 11 hectares and has a 1.5-kilometre waterfront promenade, which makes it a great place for walking, jogging, or cycling while enjoying the sea breeze.
One of the most interesting features of the park is the 400-metre Woodlands Waterfront Jetty, which is the northernmost point of mainland Singapore. Walking along the jetty is a really nice experience because you get a wide view of the sea and the coastline. Many people go there to fish, take photos, or just relax while looking at the view. In the evening, the sunset from the jetty is especially beautiful and creates a calm atmosphere.
In my opinion, the park is a great place to visit if you want to take a break from the busy city. The long paths are perfect for a peaceful walk, and the cool sea breeze makes the environment very comfortable. There are also open spaces where families can have picnics, as well as a playground where children can play and climb.
Another thing I like about the park is that it is usually less crowded compared to many other parks in Singapore. Because of this, it feels quieter and more relaxing. Overall, Woodlands Waterfront Park is a wonderful place to enjoy nature, spend time with family and friends, and appreciate the beautiful waterfront scenery.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text:
This seaside park has an interesting history. The jetty, which is the centerpiece of the park, was a mooring location for the Malayan/Malaysian Navy when Singapore was still within the federation. It's surprising that the plot of land was still in the hand of Malaysian government till it's handed over in 1980s (almost 20 years upon the secession). The bollards at the jetty point out to the place's not-so-distant past.

Since the construction of the RTS train, it has been pretty popular place for the locals to take selfie, with the backdrop of the viaduct. Funnily enough, in spite of advisory against fishing at the jetty (posted all over), seems this is just a live and let live policy lol.

place_query_type: parks gardens nature waterfront park connectors
review_text: It's a beautiful place we can see Singapore and Malaysia at that same time. When I went it's full rainy and I took photoshoot there. We want to spend peace area I will suggest The Woodlands Waterfront. Beautiful silent area. We will hear only Air sound, Water sound.

review_text:
Here's a review you can use:

---

Ryan chou
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Woodlands Waterfront Park is one of my favorite spots to unwind. The park offers a beautiful, relaxing view of the Johor Strait, especially during sunset. The waterfront promenade is perfect for a peaceful stroll or a bike ride, and there’s plenty of open space for picnics with family and friends.

I also love the iconic jetty—it’s a great place to take in the sea breeze and watch the boats go by. The park is well-maintained, with clean paths and plenty of benches to sit and enjoy the scenery. Highly recommended for anyone looking to escape the city hustle without going too far!

review_text: It is a great place to be with friends. We can enjoy the grennery and see the sea view of malaysua from here. It is quite hot in the afternoon so pls plan your trip here in the morning where it is not so hot. There is some bus stop near here but you can also walk here from woodlands mrt throguh Admiralty park. Not many facilities here except for the seafood restaurant and just greenery around.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
Love the wide variety of food and at most stalls you can really taste the years of experience and perfection of the dishes.

I enjoyed the chicken hor fun to the max. The chicken was crispy and seasoned perfectly and the noodles got to sitting the rich broth that was cooking for hours already. Loved every bite of it! Finished it off with an iced milo and it made my day after a long day of traveling.

It has the feeling of a classic Asian food court, but its not very well connected and you'd better get there by cab, if one is willing to take you. Not all shops are open everyday so it makes sense to check out before what you might wanna have

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text:
May 2026 — I revisited this market and food centre, and despite the passing years, it feels much the same. The only noticeable improvement is the larger, cleaner public toilet.
As the gateway to Pulau Tekong and Pulau Ubin, and with the bus terminal located right next to it, this is an important stop for visitors to shop and enjoy a meal before continuing their journey.
The atmosphere still carries a strong old kampong spirit, and many of the food stalls here are well known. I look forward to returning again soon.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Wanton mee was good. There was a very similar competitor right opposite with the same decoration and all. Maybe we had the right wanton mee! Chicken wing was not there. It was supposed to be a rather simple dish I supposed but it went so wrong.

review_text: I cant say much, only had 1 order of a very small serve of bad oyster egg, starch filled and very soggy. It's so horrible I'm appal this lousy shop is still running. Cost $6 throwing out $3.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
review_text: Ah.... Changi Village Hawker Centre, the land of nasi lemak. Fullstop. Thats right, Changi Village Hawker Centre has nothing much going for it. This hawker centre has so many stalls serving the same thing that you will cringe(yes, looking at you nasi lemak). Problem is, the nasi lemak isn't even good, and no serious foodie will recommend a nasi lemak stall here to a foreign tourist. A nasi lemak chicken wing set here cost $5, which is more expensive than most places. Taste is seriously lacking compared to other places and some stall dont even have ikan bilis. The best nasi lemak i have tasted here is Mizzy, and they have a whooping google rating of 1.9 when I reviewed them. They now have a rating of 2.0(no need to thank me). I can't seriously recommend this place to anyone.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I read the reviews before coming here today. I didn't manage to go upstairs to have a visit. Visited the level 4 today. The design looked good, although there is not much table seat for you, there are a lot of sofa for you to enjoy your reading time. The most important thing is I managed to find a few interesting books here that I like. Unlike tampines regional library which is bigger with nothing much suitable for me to browse, I will visit again here definitely. However I did check with staff here where is the place I can put the books after I read, I was surprised she asked me can put around later they will clear it. I'm not used to it maybe can have a rack or trolley for us to put the books after browsing? You can see some people come sitting down and playing with their phone, take a rest and some even sleeping on sofa. Otherwise is a nice place to have a good time of reading.

-
Visited today 19 march
I was at quiet reading zone as mentioned. However there are some people still playing video without using earpiece. Quite disturbing and no staff is stopping this person from doing it.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I have also been here before and there are limited seats available.

Note:
1.Only level 4 library compound and upstairs mainly for teenage " not suitable for adults"
2. The staff is polite and helpful If you notice noise here please alert them
3. "Thank you" is a more powerful word than ever as they know you appreciate them when you are being served.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
It's a relatively okay-sized library, although the main one at Bugis will still be the go to. This public library here will make a good start for people who can and want to devour books, before upgrading to the central library down at Bugis.

They do have quite a huge section for kids, which is great because it'd service the huge heartland population around here. Quite a few people also come in here for the air conditioning and the sofa couches, to just relax and chill.

I like that they have the last floor of White Sands, so it becomes a no brainer for anyone to circle through the floors or to take the lift all the way to where it matters.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

It was my first time going to the Pasir Ris library, but I was quite surprised at how many topics they had. Art, culture, fiction, non-fiction, thriller, mystery, horror, travel, and more. There were two levels. The second level had the teen section. The first level had a quiet reading lounge. The location is a bit far. Depending on where you are, of course. But if you stay close to Pasir Ris, the location is really good because you just have to go out of exit A, and the mall in which the library is right in front of the MRT station. They probably have what you are interested in since there is such a big selection of books. The Pasir Ris library is a library I would go to with a group of friends and just sit down and read. I would recommend this library for people that maybe want to learn something or just read something. Studying or working on your computer is also an option in the quiet reading lounge. The location at the same time is kind of nice when you're actually at the library because if you want to eat a snack or a meal, you just have to go downstairs because it's a mall. Overall, the library does have a good selection and a good location once you're actually at the library.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Not a great selection of books to begin with. Very stuffy. The air-conditioning in the mall is so much more comfortable. Left within 20 minutes as I was close to perspiring.

It's a relatively okay-sized library, although the main one at Bugis will still be the go to. This public library here will make a good start for people who can and want to devour books, before upgrading to the central library down at Bugis.

They do have quite a huge section for kids, which is great because it'd service the huge heartland population around here. Quite a few people also come in here for the air conditioning and the sofa couches, to just relax and chill.

I like that they have the last floor of White Sands, so it becomes a no brainer for anyone to circle through the floors or to take the lift all the way to where it matters.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
My rating: 4/5
What I like in libraries: Books that are available that I originally have on my online To-Be-Read (TBR). Spacious environments. Multiple book genres.

It was my first time going to the Pasir Ris library, but I was quite surprised at how many topics they had. Art, culture, fiction, non-fiction, thriller, mystery, horror, travel, and more. There were two levels. The second level had the teen section. The first level had a quiet reading lounge. The location is a bit far. Depending on where you are, of course. But if you stay close to Pasir Ris, the location is really good because you just have to go out of exit A, and the mall in which the library is right in front of the MRT station. They probably have what you are interested in since there is such a big selection of books. The Pasir Ris library is a library I would go to with a group of friends and just sit down and read. I would recommend this library for people that maybe want to learn something or just read something. Studying or working on your computer is also an option in the quiet reading lounge. The location at the same time is kind of nice when you're actually at the library because if you want to eat a snack or a meal, you just have to go downstairs because it's a mall. Overall, the library does have a good selection and a good location once you're actually at the library.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text: Not a great selection of books to begin with. Very stuffy. The air-conditioning in the mall is so much more comfortable. Left within 20 minutes as I was close to perspiring.

place_query_type: education facilities schools childcare libraries
review_text:
I read the reviews before coming here today. I didn't manage to go upstairs to have a visit. Visited the level 4 today. The design looked good, although there is not much table seat for you, there are a lot of sofa for you to enjoy your reading time. The most important thing is I managed to find a few interesting books here that I like. Unlike tampines regional library which is bigger with nothing much suitable for me to browse, I will visit again here definitely. However I did check with staff here where is the place I can put the books after I read, I was surprised she asked me can put around later they will clear it. I'm not used to it maybe can have a rack or trolley for us to put the books after browsing? You can see some people come sitting down and playing with their phone, take a rest and some even sleeping on sofa. Otherwise is a nice place to have a good time of reading.

review_text:
Pasir Panjang Mrt
Rating 4.8

Today Sylvia Ang known  as Ang Lay Kheng took the Pasir Panjang Mrt
Circle line to Serangoon North MRT.

The control station was well maintained. The staff are polite & answered passengers inquiry promptly.

Kudos & thanks to the management, the station is clean & well maintained.

It takes 30 mins
Was a smooth ride
The train is not so busy. So be able to find a seat.

The station have brought easier access to these 2 location. Servicing the seamen as well as workers in PSA and the general public to Pasir Panjang Food Centre.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Pasir Ris Hawker Centre
review_text:
23 Oct 2025, 4.20pm. 3 stars.

Well, I can't understand the hype or the 5 star reviews given for this brand.

Their min jiang kueh is definitely not the best I've had. I ordered the original red bean $1.60 and there was no queue, no wait. Female staff was quite efficient & responsive to questions.

The hardened edges were like eating fortune cookies, which I quite like, but the baked surfaces of the pastry was a bit too hard & chewy. Inner texture was a little bit softer. Overall can be lighter / crispier. Red bean paste was fragrant, a good amount, but could be less sweet. It got a bit jelak as I was finishing the pancake.

Noticed that the portion size was a bit smaller for the green tea skins compared to the original & charcoal skins.

CDC vouchers accepted for payment.

Will only buy if in the vicinity & have a craving.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Pasir Ris Hawker Centre
review_text: This place is pretty airy and the environment is great. The food is hot and tastes good with plentiful of peanuts.

review_text: The filling is overflowing and they can heat up for you! Best ! Value for money

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Pasir Ris Hawker Centre
review_text: Great options of pancakes and fillings. Good service from staff. Crowd was managed efficiently during busy periods with a great smile especially by the lady staff who I believe is the boss as she was the only one not in uniform. Overall, one of my favorite Munchi branches.

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Pasir Ris Hawker Centre
review_text: i would like to praise one of the worker name yusrah. she is very outgoing and patient when i was taking order from her. she even suggested which one is nicer and her taste is definitely good. will come back here agan !

place_query_type: food dining hawker centres restaurants cafes
place_name: Munchi Pancakes @ Pasir Ris Hawker Centre
review_text:
23 Oct 2025, 4.20pm. 3 stars.

Well, I can't understand the hype or the 5 star reviews given for this brand.

Their min jiang kueh is definitely not the best I've had. I ordered the original red bean $1.60 and there was no queue, no wait. Female staff was quite efficient & responsive to questions.

The hardened edges were like eating fortune cookies, which I quite like, but the baked surfaces of the pastry was a bit too hard & chewy. Inner texture was a little bit softer. Overall can be lighter / crispier. Red bean paste was fragrant, a good amount, but could be less sweet. It got a bit jelak as I was finishing the pancake.

Noticed that the portion size was a bit smaller for the green tea skins compared to the original & charcoal skins.

CDC vouchers accepted for payment.

Will only buy if in the vicinity & have a craving.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: Pasir Ris MRT Station is clean, convenient, and well-connected. Located next to Pasir Ris Mall and White Sands, it offers easy access to shopping, buses, and recreational areas like Pasir Ris Park. The station is spacious with good ventilation, lifts, and clear signage. Trains run frequently, and recent upgrades have improved travel efficiency. A great starting point for both daily commutes and weekend outings.

place_query_type: public transport MRT LRT bus stops
review_text: The terminal station was quite quiet when we arrived at 3.45pm this. The place was very clean and well laid out, with clear signage and escalator for elderly and the physically impaired. We get out of the station efficiently. It is also well connected to the White Sand Mall & bus interchange with seamless connectivity, which really helps because we were in a rush to visit a friend in Pasir Ris by bus. Love it.
